title: France, Switzerland & Italy 1993: Guincourt, the St Gotthard, the Italian Lakes & Liguria

main_route.excerpt: We went to church at Rochester Cathedral. Simon had telephoned ahead to find the time of the service (10.30.)
We left straight after receiving Communion to get to Dover. It was an uneventful crossing.
We reached Guincourt before dark. John + Charles loved the playground. We met a family from Newtownards.

main_route.original_wording: Withers, Le Long Pres / Au Lion de Belfort, Than
main_route.excerpt: We spent the night at Withers, Le Long Pres. For supper we went to Than to the Au Lion de Belfort.
We drove of past Lucerne + over the St. Gottard.
We drove of past Lucerne + over the St. Gottard. Snow, old paved stone & cobbled road with pink stripe down the middle. We got out + walked around.
About 18 miles south of the pass we came to the St. Gottard campsite. It was perched on the side of a hill.
We packed up at lunch time + set off for Lugano. It's a very pretty town with a tree park + playground.
We turned off to Camping OK Porlezza + swam in Lake Piano, between Lugano + Como.
We set off + stopped at Crema + saw the Duomo with an enormous façade, much bigger than the cathedral inside.
On to Soncino ... At Soncino we stopped by a lovely castle + moat + the boys played football with a group of local boys + girls.
We spent the night at the Cremona campsite, + had pizzas in a friendly restaurant where we watched them putting pizzas in the oven.
We left & passed through a spa town, Salso[?Maggiore] Terme, & found signs to a campsite out in the country.
found signs to a campsite out in the country ... we are very fortunate to be camped right on the edge with a marvellous view. Arizona
We left & went to Parma ... We camped at Parma, where there was an open air concert with a blond singer.
We had trouble finding Torrechiara. It is marvellous ... Gorgeous Romanesque arches + pleasing proportions in the open courtyard.
At the far side we came down to Berceto. A lovely town with a beautiful cathedral - plain Romanesque.

main_route.normalized_name: Borgo Val di Taro, Emilia-Romagna, Italy
main_route.excerpt: On towards Sestri Levante. We've stopped to camp in Borgo val de Taro. Campsite in new town, we walk up the hill to the old town.
then on down to Varese, a very early example of town planning. We walked around lovely colours in the house painting.
In the afternoon we tried to camp at Livorno but there was no room.
We finally found a camp at Sestri Levante Trigoso only 300m. from a station one down from Sestri Levante, which is just what we wanted.
We got onto the autostrada + came off at Rapallo to look for a church ... Finally we found a gilded St Lawrence.
We had lunch in a layby + drove on to Cuneo, where we had trouble following signs to the campsite. The boys played ping pong.
We played ping pong + set off towards les Deux Alps. We stopped to shop at Saluzzo.
finally reached les Deux Alys[?], where the 3 boys had skied 2 years before. It was dark when we arrived. Cold, cold.
At 12.15. we left + passed Grenoble + ploughed through Lyons in the rush hour.
passed Grenoble + ploughed through Lyons in the rush hour. At the last minute we visited the Fracastos + swam, + they gave us a delicious barbequed supper.
we drove off & reached St. Jean de Vaux at 10.30. w. of Chalon sur Saone. A gorgeous vingard runs down right to the edge of the campsite.
Set off on a long drive up towards Calais 12.00 ... We reached Lumbres [?] at 9. pm. John + Charles got filthy in the play ground.

main_route.inference_reason: return Channel crossing not narrated within this spread range (mixed_end); family drove 'up towards Calais' before the bundle ends at Lumbres, so Calais is the most likely return port
main_route.excerpt: Set off on a long drive up towards Calais 12.00.

main_route.inference_reason: return crossing arrival port inferred as Dover (counterpart of outbound Dover crossing); not narrated in this spread
main_route.excerpt: Set off on a long drive up towards Calais 12.00.

crossings.excerpt: We left straight after receiving Communion to get to Dover. It was an uneventful crossing. We reached Guincourt before dark.
crossings.note: Dover named explicitly; French arrival port not named. Dover-Calais assumed as the standard England-France ferry; arrival at Guincourt (Ardennes) the same evening is consistent with Calais.

crossings.excerpt: Set off on a long drive up towards Calais 12.00.
crossings.note: Return crossing not narrated within this spread (mixed_end). Modelled as Calais-Dover (reverse of dover-calais); Calais named as the drive target, Lumbres the last recorded overnight near Calais/Saint-Omer.

excursions.label: Cinque Terre day: train to Corniglia, walk to Vernazza

stops.excerpt: We hurried + caught the 9.20. train to Corniglia, to walk back to Vernazza.
At Vernazza we had lunch overlooking an inlet of waves which had cut through a tunnel in the rock ... we swam in the harbour + caught a train home.

excursions.label: Day trip into Sestri Levante town

stops.excerpt: Simon + Thomas found a bus for us to take instead. We window shopped + saw lovely jewelry + the boys bought yet more cars.

excursions.label: Evening drive to Givry for supper (Hotel du Raisin)

stops.excerpt: At 7.30 we left for supper at "Hotel de Raisin" Givry. It's quite a large, pretty town, buildings of tired yellow.

unresolved.issue: Overnight campsite name near Thann (Haut-Rhin); 'Le Long Pre' not confidently geocodable as a settlement. Anchored to Thann (where they had supper at the 'Au Lion de Belfort').
unresolved.excerpt: We spent the night at Withers, Le Long Pres. For supper we went to Than to the Au Lion de Belfort.

unresolved.issue: Family/friends visited near Lyon for a barbecue and swim; a household, not a separate geographic stop. Folded into the Lyon main_stop rather than mapped separately.
unresolved.excerpt: At the last minute we visited the Fracastos + swam, + they gave us a delicious barbequed supper.

unresolved.issue: A wine-tasting establishment at/near Saint-Jean-de-Vaux, not a distinct place; walked to from the campsite. Not mapped as a stop.
unresolved.excerpt: Simon & Thomas found a wine tasting place & we all walked off to Marc Laborde & tasted & bought a dozen of white & red.

boundary_flags.issue: mixed_start: A_0094_L opens mid-sentence ('where Simon bought Eve a new blue handbag') describing a Rochester museum/camping-shop visit that belongs to this trip's first day (July 11). The preceding trip's narrative shares this spread.

mixed_end: the bundle ends at Lumbres on 30 July (A_0110_L) with the family still in France 'up towards Calais'. The actual Channel crossing and home return fall on pages beyond this spread (A_0110_R is a caption page that already refers to the Chalons-sur-Saone leg). Return crossing and home stop are therefore inferred.

summary: A long July 1993 driving holiday from Birmingham through France, Switzerland and Italy and back. The family crossed Dover-Calais to Guincourt, drove via Alsace (Thann) over the St Gotthard Pass to the Italian Lakes (Porlezza, Lugano), then south through Lombardy and Emilia-Romagna (Cremona, the 'Arizona' campsite near Salsomaggiore, Parma/Torrechiara) to the Ligurian coast at Sestri Levante, with Cinque Terre excursions (Corniglia, Vernazza). They returned via Cuneo and the Alps to Les Deux Alpes, then Burgundy (Saint-Jean-de-Vaux, with suppers at Givry) before the long run north to Lumbres near Calais.
extraction_notes: Sketch pages A_0095_R and A_0097_L are scenery/landscape sketches (the St Gotthard Pass road; the campsite view at Porlezza), NOT route maps or numbered route lists, so evidence-hierarchy rule 1 does not override the dated narrative here; they do corroborate St Gotthard and Porlezza. Dates: only A_0101 onward carry explicit ISO dates (19 July and later); earlier July 11/12 and the 'Wednesday/Thursday/Friday' headings were chained into 1993 calendar dates (11 Jul=Sun, 14 Jul=Wed Lugano, 15 Jul=Thu Crema/Soncino->Cremona, 16 Jul=Fri ->Arizona). 'Varese' (seq 20) is normalized to Varese Ligure (Liguria) rather than Varese (Lombardy): the caption 'early town planning, bridge 1519' matches Varese Ligure's circular 'borgo rotondo', and it sits on-route between Borgo Val di Taro and the Ligurian coast; flagged with reduced confidence. Torrechiara is treated as a main_stop on the departure leg from Parma (they did not return to the Parma base; they continued over the Sillara pass to Berceto and Borgo Val di Taro), not an excursion. Country list reflects GB, FR, CH (Lucerne/Gotthard/Lugano) and IT. The trip almost certainly continues beyond this spread; return crossing and home arrival are inferred low-confidence.
